reason: ets.new/2's contract returns atom()|tid() because it cannot express option-dependence; this gold creates an UNNAMED table, so the tid() spec is truthful and dialyzer's 'might also return atom()' is unreachable. Triage 2026-07-16 (Claude, Kamil-delegated).

base32_value/1's spec 0..31 is truthful: both clauses are guarded char ranges (?A..?Z -> 0..25, ?2..?7 -> 26..31). Dialyzer widens the guarded char arithmetic to byte() and, on the 2026-07-17 pass, even to 'might also return float()' - both unreachable. Spec kept tight on purpose. Triage 2026-07-17 (Claude, Kamil-delegated T1.6 queue).
